title: Rich Turkish Myeongnan Roe Stew
description: A sour and velvety one-pot Turkish dish featuring myeongnan-roe, this stew is a low-fat friendly option for a weeknight dinner. It is poached to perfection and full of flavor.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Stir in the turkish red pepper paste and cook for 1 minute.
Add the chicken broth, myeongnan-roe, lemon juice, salt, and black pepper to the pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes.
Use two forks to poach the myeongnan-roe and break it up into smaller pieces.
Continue to simmer the stew for an additional 10 minutes or until the myeongnan-roe is cooked through.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
